context.meal: buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, and ranch dressing
context.setting: Watching TV with TV on Left

episode_text:
day=8; meal=buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, and ranch dressing; setting=Watching TV with TV on Left; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize left occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is fruit or other cold-served food AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context, THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user explicitly chose no reheating for a fruit breakfast. IF food_condition is a warm savory meal AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person on the right, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected a longer reheat time for a hot savory meal in that social context. IF food_condition is a warm savory meal AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front or on the left, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose a longer reheat time for savory meals while working. IF food_condition is a mixed meal including brownies or sauce-relevant items AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected reheating for that TV meal, indicating preference for serving non-fruit breakfast items warm. IF food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the left, THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user selected the longest reheating time in that left-TV savory lunch, indicating a stronger hot-food preference in that context.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly prioritized preserving visibility toward the right-side TV. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user again prioritized keeping the right side visually open for social engagement. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user chose to preserve the front visual field while using a front-facing device. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected front-occlusion minimization while attending to a front-facing TV. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly protected the left visual field when the attention target was on the left. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal, including savory meals with dip or sauce, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly did not request left-side visibility protection in that left-laptop context, suggesting occlusion sensitivity tracks the salience of the attended target rather than simply its direction.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a light cold fruit meal, THEN fast BECAUSE the user explicitly chose faster robot motion in that simple meal context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a mixed or more complex meal, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected a more moderate speed for a more complex TV meal. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is on the left, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal, THEN medium BECAUSE the user consistently chose medium speed in repeated laptop-engaged savory meals. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected slower motion in that left-TV lunch, indicating greater caution or comfort-seeking for a hot, sauce-involving meal in that context.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF feeding foods that require fork skewering AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is typical skewered solid foods such as meats, nuggets, or potato pieces, THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user has limited mouth opening and repeatedly selected the narrower-bite option. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is soft dessert-like items such as brownies, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user explicitly chose the higher-acquisition-success option in that dessert meal, suggesting willingness to trade bite narrowness for acquisition reliability for soft foods.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a skewered solid food meal, THEN yes BECAUSE the user explicitly chose confirmation in a social context, consistent with preferring extra verification when failed transfers would be more awkward. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front or on the left, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a skewered solid or savory meal, including sauce-accompanied savory meals, THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose explicit bite-acquisition confirmation while working, indicating a stable preference for verification in attention-divided contexts. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a light cold fruit meal, THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in that simpler meal, consistent with preferring efficiency when acquisition is likely straightforward. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a mixed or sauce-involving meal, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected confirmation for the more complex meal, indicating that food complexity can trigger a preference for verification even in non-social media contexts. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN yes BECAUSE the user again chose confirmation in a more complex TV meal, reinforcing that complexity and divided attention favor explicit verification.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any previously observed routine, social, laptop, or front-TV context AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any previously observed food condition, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user can lean forward comfortably and has usually selected the less intrusive transfer style. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user explicitly switched to direct delivery in that context, indicating that when attention is directed to a side TV during a more complex meal the user may prefer reduced self-motion and more robot assistance.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN not applicable BECAUSE outside-mouth stopping distance does not apply when the robot delivers directly into the mouth. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is a routine meal AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stopping distance in the routine setting. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person on the right AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN medium BECAUSE the user chose a slightly larger buffer in that social setting. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front or on the left, AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN near BECAUSE the user repeatedly returned to the closer stopping distance in laptop-engaged contexts. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN near BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred the closer stopping distance in TV contexts.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a routine quiet meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN speech BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred spoken readiness cues in the routine setting. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user switched to a quieter visual cue in the social setting. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front or on the left, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred a visual cue while engaged with a device. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a visual cue while attending to TV. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected a multimodal cue in that divided-attention context, indicating preference for a more noticeable signal when attention is directed leftward.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any previously observed routine, social, laptop, or front-TV context AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control, can easily press buttons, and limited mouth opening makes open-mouth readiness less suitable; the user has consistently selected explicit manual signaling across those contexts. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly shifted to lower-effort automation in that TV context, suggesting that when attention is occupied by side viewing the user may prefer the robot to proceed automatically rather than require manual input.

IF dining context is any previously observed routine, social, laptop, or front-TV context AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control and has consistently preferred explicit manual readiness signaling for drinking across those contexts. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly chose automatic progression in that divided-attention TV context, indicating a situational preference for reduced interaction effort.

IF dining context is any previously observed routine, social, laptop, or front-TV context AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control and has consistently preferred explicit manual readiness signaling for mouth wiping across those contexts. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly chose automatic progression in that divided-attention TV context, indicating a situational preference for reduced interaction effort.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a routine quiet meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN speech BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred spoken completion cues in the routine setting. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user switched to a visual completion cue in the social setting. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front or on the left, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred a visual completion cue while engaged with a device. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a visual completion cue while watching TV. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected a multimodal completion cue in that divided-attention context, indicating preference for a more salient signal when attention is directed away from the robot.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected the reliable automatic completion method for bites across contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any previously observed routine, social, laptop, or front-TV context AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user consistently preferred explicit manual completion signaling rather than automatic completion across those contexts. IF drinking task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the left AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly chose automatic completion in that divided-attention TV context, indicating a situational shift toward lower-effort automation for non-feeding tasks.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF mouth wiping task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any previously observed routine, social, laptop, or front-TV context AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user consistently preferred explicit manual completion signaling rather than automatic completion across those contexts. IF mouth wiping task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the left AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly chose automatic completion in that divided-attention TV context, indicating a situational shift toward lower-effort automation for non-feeding tasks.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user chose to reduce prolonged visual obstruction in a context where right-side visibility matters. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user again chose retraction when right-side engagement mattered. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user accepted longer meal time to reduce obstruction while maintaining visibility of a front-facing task. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose retraction when front visibility mattered. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN yes BECAUSE the user also chose retraction when a left-side attention target was present, reinforcing that salient visual engagement in any direction can motivate retraction. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal, including savory meals with dip or sauce, THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly did not request retraction in that left-laptop context, suggesting retraction depends on how strongly the user wants to preserve a particular visual channel rather than on leftward attention alone.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is a meal without sauces or dips AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping when dipping was unnecessary. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front with a simple fruit meal, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to avoid dipping despite sauce being available, consistent with an efficiency or low-mess preference in that meal. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front or on the left, THEN less BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose some dipping but limited the amount, suggesting a mess-averse compromise while multitasking. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front with a mixed meal including dessert or sauce-relevant items, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected limited dipping rather than none, indicating a preference for modest flavor addition without heavy mess. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the left with a savory meal, THEN less BECAUSE the user again chose limited dipping, reinforcing a stable preference for restrained sauce use in attention-divided contexts.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a routine quiet meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user preferred a short timeout, consistent with efficient progression when explicit button controls are available. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user chose a very long timeout in the social setting, indicating a strong preference to avoid unwanted automatic progression when attention may be shared. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front or on the left, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose a very long timeout while working, indicating preference to prevent automatic progression when attention is divided. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a light cold fruit meal, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the short timeout in that simpler TV context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a more complex mixed or sauce-involving meal, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long timeout there, indicating that in TV contexts food complexity can shift the user toward stronger protection against unwanted automatic continuation. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected a short timeout while also choosing several autocontinue-based controls, indicating a context-specific preference for efficient automatic flow rather than long interruption windows.
